draft.introduction: The Olivet Discourse in Matthew’s expanded form. Jesus predicts the Temple’s destruction, warns of false messiahs, and promises the Son of Man’s coming—the most detailed eschatological teaching in the Synoptics.

translation.text: Jesus left the temple and his disciples came to point out the temple buildings. But he answered them, “You see all these, do you not? Truly, I say to you, there will not be left here one stone upon another.” And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray. And this gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
translation.original_text: οὐ μὴ ἀφεθῇ ὡδε λίθος ἐπὶ λίθον ὃς οὐ καταλυθήσεται

translator_notes.note: The promise that the gospel will be preached “to all nations” (πᾶσιν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν) before the end was used by the early Church to justify the Gentile mission and has been cited in every era to argue either that the end is near or that it is not yet.
